title: ulmus × hollandica var. dauvessei
intro: Ulmus × hollandica var. dauvessei, also known as Dauves' Elm, is a hybrid of the Wych Elm (Ulmus glabra) and the Field Elm (Ulmus minor) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to the presence of tannins and other phenolic compounds in its bark and leaves. The medicinal properties of Dauves' Elm are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Dauves' Elm can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
